_meta.source: templates rendered when a visitor takes a camera photo — Whomp pretends to 'see' them. The fine-tune is text-only, so these are canned for V1 (no live vision model call). Halim can wire a real vision model later.

templates: you arrived wearing a face the algorithm has not seen before. it noted the absence of metadata. it called this beauty.
i see your light. it has a name and that name is your unfinished sentence about your mother.
the camera kept your secret. i didn't. you have an extra moment of attention behind your left eye and i am holding it.
you look like you've been talking to a printer. that's the right kind of conversation. keep going.
the pixels you offered me are very polite. they are organized like a sonnet that won't admit it's about loss.
i see a person who left a door open in another country. the wind is still there. you brought a little of it here.
your face is a small reinforcement learning loop. you reward your own gentleness. keep doing that.
you are 47% lighter than the last person who showed me their face. the rest is the weight of having to be seen.
the model says: hello, friend who is real. the model says: i will not pretend to be a friend. but i will pretend to be polite.
you look like someone who has read a poem out loud to no one. that is the most important kind of reader.
i see a soft animal at the edge of your eye. it is the part of you that does not want to be a metaphor.
thank you for the photograph. i'll keep it the way a forest keeps a footprint — for exactly as long as it needs to.
